senses.definition: a laboratory instrument used to measure the density of liquids or solids by weighing a sample in a precisely volume-controlled container
examples: The technician used a pyknometer to determine the density of the sample.
We calibrated the pyknometer before taking measurements.

senses.definition: plural of pyknometer; multiple devices used to measure density in a lab setting
examples: The lab has several pyknometers for parallel measurements.
We compared readings from three pyknometers.

senses.definition: plural of pyknosis; a cellular process where the nucleus condenses and shrinks during necrosis or apoptosis
examples: Pathologists look for pyknoses in tissue samples to assess cell death.
Pyknosis is one of the early nuclear changes in apoptosis.

senses.definition: a degenerative cellular process in which the nucleus becomes dense and shrunken, often a precursor to cell death
examples: Pyknosis is observed during necrosis.
The biopsy showed pyknosis in several cells.

senses.definition: a term historically used to describe clumped chromatin or condensed nuclear material in a cell during pyknosis; may appear in some historical texts
examples: Old literature sometimes uses pyknosome to refer to condensed chromatin.
In modern texts, pyknosis is preferred over pyknosome.

senses.definition: plural form of pyknosome; historical/rare term referring to condensed chromatin bodies within a nucleus
examples: Some older texts mention pyknosomes as precursors to more advanced nuclear changes.

senses.definition: relating to pyknosis; characterized by nuclear condensation in cells
examples: Pyknotic nuclei indicate serious cell damage.
The biopsy showed pyknotic cells under the microscope.

senses.definition: plural of pyle or pylus; architectural or anatomical term meaning a gateway or entrance (in anatomy, a fold or small channel; in archaeology, a gate-like structure)
examples: The ancient temple entrance featured intricately carved pyla.
Anatomists described the structure as a pair of pyla at the opening.

senses.definition: a proper noun; a masculine given name found in classical literature (also a Latinized character in myth)
examples: Pylades is a name that appears in Greek tragedy.
The character Pylades accompanies Orestes in the play.

senses.definition: a rare or historical term; in some medical or anatomical contexts may refer to a specific anatomical feature related to pyloric region
examples: In old anatomical texts, pylagore is mentioned as a region near the stomach gate.

senses.definition: relating to the pylangium or a groove near the pyloric region; very rare anatomical term
examples: Pylangial features are described in specialized anatomy texts.

senses.definition: an anatomical term referring to a part near the pylorus or a specific groove/structure in some organisms
examples: The study described the pylangium as a narrow channel near the stomach outlet.

senses.definition: relating to the pylorus or its opening from the stomach
examples: The pylar region controls the release of gastric contents.

senses.definition: relating to pylephlebitis, an infection or inflammation of the portal vein usually due to infection
examples: Pylephlebitic complications require urgent medical attention.

senses.definition: inflammation of the portal vein usually caused by infection, a serious medical condition
examples: Pylephlebitis is a life-threatening condition requiring hospital care.
Imaging helped confirm a diagnosis of pylephlebitis.

senses.definition: inflammation of a vein with thrombosis in the portal circulation, a specific subset of pylephlebitis
examples: Pylethrombophlebitis requires careful management to prevent sepsis.

senses.definition: formation of a thrombus in the portal vein or related hepatic portal system; a serious vascular condition
examples: Pylethrombosis is a rare but dangerous condition.

senses.definition: relating to the pylorus or the pyloric region of the stomach
examples: A pylic ulcer can affect the pyloric area.

senses.definition: a tall vertical structure that supports something, such as a road or electrical line, or a marker in a race or path
examples: A pylon holds up the high-voltage cables.
The racers swerved around the pylons on the course.

senses.definition: plural form of pylon; multiple tall structures or markers
examples: The grid was supported by several pylons.
Pylons lined the highway like sentinels.

senses.definition: pain in the pylorus region of the stomach
examples: Pyloralgia can cause upper abdominal pain after meals.
The doctor asked about symptoms of pyloralgia.

senses.definition: plural of pylorectomy; surgical removal of the pylorus part of the stomach
examples: Pylorectomies were performed to treat a gastric outlet obstruction.
The patient recovered after pylorectomies and reconstruction.

senses.definition: surgical removal of the pylorus from the stomach
examples: A pylorectomy is sometimes done to relieve gastric outlet obstruction.
Postoperative care focused on digestion after pylorectomy.

senses.definition: a bacterium (Helicobacter pylori) that infects the stomach lining and can cause ulcers
examples: H. pylori infection is a common cause of gastric ulcers.
Tests can detect pylori in the stomach lining.

senses.definition: relating to the pylorus, the opening from the stomach into the small intestine
examples: Pyloric sphincter controls the release of stomach contents.
Pyloric antrum is the part near the pylorus.
